_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Process data access requests per defined procedures, including request validation, approval routing, provisioning, and access log documentation.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-T-015 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.404), DA-T-009 (DA, cosine: 0.400).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.400). Shared vocabulary: [requests]. DCWF-only terms [analyze, collection, incoming] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, approval, data, defined, documentation, log] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.914, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.059 — the DCWF element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:1.2%, N:68.3%, C:30.5%). No dominant classification for "Analyze incoming collection requests." vs "Process data access requests per defined procedures, including request validatio" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.447 ≈ STS 0.400 (gap 0.047). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [requests] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.3%. Shared tokens: [requests]. DCWF-only: [analyze, collection, incoming]. WIDAI-only: [access, approval, data, defined, documentation, log].
